headings.text: We build identities that hold their breath.
Three clients per quarter, no more.
The studio answers its own phone.

paragraphs.text: Obsidian Fold is a three-person studio working between cultural institutions and independent luxury labels. We treat identity as architecture — load-bearing, deliberate, slow. Each system we ship is engineered to outlast trend cycles, refusing decoration in favour of geometry, restraint, and the precision of a well-folded sheet of paper.
Twelve years building narrative frameworks for museums and heritage labels across DACH. Maren writes the brief no one knew they needed, then defends it.
Former art director at Sulki & Min. Dae-Jung architects grids, marks, and modular systems with the patience of a stonemason and the ear of a typographer.
Trained at Reading. Ines draws letters for a living and animates them only when the argument demands it. Custom type director on every project.
Scarcity is not a tactic; it is the only condition under which we can defend the work.
We begin with a paid discovery week. Either we earn the engagement or we part on good terms.
Every system ships with at least one drawn glyph form. Identity that cannot be set in metal cannot be set at all.
Account management is a role we have refused since 2017. You speak to the people drawing the marks.
Boutique brand identity for cultural institutions and independent luxury labels. Zieglergasse 14, 1070 Wien.

visibleText: Obsidian Fold Manifesto Work Process Studio Contact Studio · Est. 2017 · Vienna A small room for large ideas. Founded 2017 Based Wien VII Principals Three Practice Identity Ethos We build identities that hold their breath. Obsidian Fold is a three-person studio working between cultural institutions and independent luxury labels. We treat identity as architecture — load-bearing, deliberate, slow. Each system we ship is engineered to outlast trend cycles, refusing decoration in favour of geometry, restraint, and the precision of a well-folded sheet of paper. ZIEGLERGASSE 14 The Three. Principals · Partners · Practice Leads Maren Scholz Strategy Twelve years building narrative frameworks for museums and heritage labels across DACH. Maren writes the brief no one knew they needed, then defends it. Dae-Jung Yoo Visual Systems Former art director at Sulki & Min. Dae-Jung architects grids, marks, and modular systems with the patience of a stonemason and the ear of a typographer. Ines Varela Type & Motion Trained at Reading. Ines draws letters for a living and animates them only when the argument demands it. Custom type director on every project. House Rules — 04 01 Three clients per quarter, no more. Scarcity is not a tactic; it is the only condition under which we can defend the work. 02 No pitches. No spec work. We begin with a paid discovery week. Either we earn the engagement or we part on good terms. 03 Custom type by default. Every system ships with at least one drawn glyph form. Identity that cannot be set in metal cannot be set at all. 04 The studio answers its own phone. Account management is a role we have refused since 2017. You speak to the people drawing the marks.
